mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as earnings expectations are downgraded over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances where unemployment jumps exceed the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a positive return probability above 55 percent over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced economic activity, causing short-term equity depreciation within the next week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during industrial production contractions across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price indices breach the 335.0 threshold, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of observations above the threshold over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate earnings trajectories and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return probability drops below 50 percent in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to macro output, prompting earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive returns in greater than 55 percent of cases over 20-day horizons during tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above the 103.0 index level confirms robust manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Five-day returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of threshold-crossing events across multiple market cycles.
